reasoning: With the tragic death of Kelvin Kiptum (the current world record holder at 2:00:35) in early 2024, the marathon world lost the only athlete visibly threatening to break the 2-hour barrier in a legal race. Throughout 2025, the fastest runners have posted times closer to 2:02. For example, the 2025 Berlin Marathon was won in 2:02:16, Chicago in 2:02:23, and New York in 2:08:09. Only a few weeks remain in 2025, with the Valencia Marathon being the only significant event left that might see extremely fast times. It is phenomenally unlikely that any active runner will suddenly shave nearly two minutes off the times we've seen this year to break 2 hours legally. Furthermore, any special 'moonshot' attempt (similar to INEOS 1:59) would likely not meet World Athletics standards for a ratified record. Accordingly, the probability of a legal sub-2 occurring before December 31, 2025, is practically zero.
